---
title: "Sandswamp Whitetop Sedge (Rhynchospora latifolia)"
slug: "rhynchospora-latifolia"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180793088/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Sandswamp Whitetop Sedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180793088/medium.jpg" alt="Sandswamp Whitetop Sedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/alt_janel">
alt_janel</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/149591711">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=167879)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Rare species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as bogs, and wet flatwoods.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like wetland plant characterized by its broader leaves and distinctive white, leaf-like bracts surrounding its flower clusters. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and seepage areas, often in sandy soils. It grows during the warmer months and is most noticeable when in flower, contributing to the visual and structural diversity of wetland plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/167879-Rhynchospora-latifolia)
-   [Local profile](https://www.fnps.org/plant/rhynchospora-latifolia#gsc.tab=0)
